title: 10 precautions to take when using Salvia miltiorrhiza (Dan Shen) for medicinal purposes
intro: Salvia miltiorrhiza, also known as Danshen, is a plant commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. When using Salvia miltiorrhiza for medicinal purposes, certain precautions should be taken. First, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, as Danshen may interact with other medications and have potential side effects. Pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using Danshen, as its safety during pregnancy and lactation has not been established. It is also recommended to use standardized extracts of Danshen, as the quality and purity of raw herbs can vary significantly. Overdosing on Danshen should be avoided, as it can lead to symptoms such as dizziness, reduced heart rate, and low blood pressure. Lastly, it is crucial to store Danshen in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight and out of reach of children and pets.
